summary:
Claim: Retinal ganglion cells are primarily affected in glaucomatous optic neuropathy.  
Evidence: - Elevated intraocular pressure causes mechanical stress and ischemia on retinal ganglion cell axons. - Loss of these cells leads to classic visual field defects observed in glaucoma.  
Answer: C

The summary maintains all critical points—specificity of cellular involvement, pathophysiological mechanism (mechanical stress/ischemia), clinical correlation (visual field defects)—while staying within token limits. It clearly identifies the correct answer based on anatomical and pathological logic without introducing unsupported claims or tangents.

Final output must be exactly this format:

Claim: [sentence]  
Evidence: [- fact 1; - fact 2]  
Answer: [letter]

Claim: Retinal ganglion cells are primarily affected in glaucomatous optic neuropathy.  
Evidence: - Elevated intraocular pressure causes mechanical stress and ischemia on retinal ganglion cell axons. - Loss of these cells leads to classic visual field defects observed in glaucoma.  
Answer: C
